If you thought Netflix was the start of only movies and shows we'd consume regularly with monthly payments, we can now add gaming to the list.

Retro gaming is making a comeback of sorts. Mini PCs are proving invaluable for launching classic games on older-generation consoles. If you have a collection of retro games in your library and would like some way to enjoy them on the big screen without messing around with the decade-old console, software such as Batocera is excellent for hits of nostalgia. And once again, we're seeing the fusion of console and PC through emulation and translation. Is the console having all the makings of a PC a bad thing? That depends on how you view the console.

item.title: 9 reasons every PC enthusiast needs a 3D printer
item.itunes:title: 9 reasons every PC enthusiast needs a 3D printer
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Hey, PC enthusiasts, have you ever considered using 3D printing to customize your PC? It’s not just for modding your gaming rig; it can also help with maintenance and upgrades. 3D printing lets you create custom parts and accessories on the fly, experiment with new case designs, and even customize your whole system to suit your unique needs.

If you’re into tinkering with hardware or just want to make your workspace more personalized, a 3D printer can be a game-changer. Here are nine reasons to consider getting one for your PC building projects.

 Related
You can now build your own 3D printer for less with this amazing open-source project

Never be stuck looking for a specific part again.

1

9 Connect with a sharing community

Find designs online and contribute your own ideas

Online repositories like Thingiverse and Printables are a treasure trove of free models for PC components and accessories. You can easily download mounts, replacement parts, or even decorative items and customize them to fit your specific build. This is especially helpful for people who want a quick solution or are still learning to use 3D modeling software.

Sharing your creations also builds a supportive and fun community among hobbyists. You can upload your custom parts, ask for feedback, and learn from experienced people. This exchange of ideas can lead to new and better ways to 3D print and build PCs.

Many people share tips on how to make your prints better and how to modify your PC. They often give advice on things like slicer settings and how to mount your GPU. By exploring these resources, you can find simple solutions to problems that are hard to solve when 3D printing.

8 Experiment with different 3D filaments

Explore advanced materials for more robust PC modifications

Standard PLA is great for many things, but special filaments can take your PC to the next level. You can use carbon fiber-infused materials to strengthen brackets, or flexible filaments like TPU to absorb vibrations near fans and mechanical drives.

Different materials also allow you to experiment with temperature, nozzle sizes, and print settings. For example, you might find that a specific filament is perfect for a hot part or that another helps reduce noise in a particular area.

Playing with different filament types teaches you how to balance performance, strength, and looks. Over time, you can start to associate specific materials with different parts of your PC, like high-load mounts or functional PC case panels. This helps you learn more and makes it easier to create more cool mods.

7 Develop essential design and CAD skills

Master software techniques for creative problem-solving in PC builds

Learning design and computer-aided design (CAD) software can boost your building skills. You can make simple changes to a bracket or create something totally new, and it’ll be so much easier if you know how to draw and measure your ideas accurately. This way, you can handle any hardware problems that don’t have a pre-made solution.

Using 3D modeling tools makes you think about how big, how close, and how well things fit together. You’ll learn how to create strong and easy-to-assemble parts with practice. This can even help you with your own projects, and you might even start sharing your creations with others. Getting feedback and new ideas can really help you improve your skills.

You might even start designing parts for friends or sharing your creations with the community as you get better. Seeing your ideas come to life for others can be truly rewarding. Over time, you’ll learn how to turn your thoughts into real things, and that’s one of the most extraordinary things about 3D printing and PC building.

6 Prototype exciting designs at home

Test and refine custom parts with quick turnaround times

Imagine having a magical tool that can turn your wildest creative ideas into physical objects in just a few hours. With a 3D printer, you can sketch your design, print it, and watch it come to life right before your eyes. This instant feedback speeds up your development process and helps you make the best decisions.

But here’s the cool part: you can keep changing and improving your design as you go. This hands-on experimentation with prototypes feels like playing with toys but can lead to some seriously innovative solutions. For example, you could try different cable routes or find a better way to mount radiators.

This practical experience also makes you a better 3D modeler. With each iteration and redesign, you learn more and more about how things work together. This practical learning experience builds confidence and helps you create better parts over time. So, if you’re up for a challenge, a 3D printer is your ticket to unlocking your creativity and making amazing things happen.

5 Replace broken parts with ease

Extend the life of components no longer in production

It can be a real pain when tiny plastic parts break on old cases or peripherals, and replacements are hard to find. However, a 3D printer can save the day. It lets you make your own stuff, like hinges, clips, or drive bay adapters that manufacturers have stopped making.

Printing your own spare parts is way faster than searching for used ones or waiting for them to ship. Once you have a good model, you can print it repeatedly, which is especially helpful if multiple parts have the same problem.

Many hobbyists keep a collection of replacement parts on hand to be ready for unexpected repairs. You can find or share these files online, which helps keep e-waste down, since there won’t be as many parts manufactured.

4 Build helpful desk and gaming accessories

Keep your workspace organized with unique printed solutions

Imagine having a 3D printer that goes beyond making simple PC upgrades. Think of your 3D printer as a personal design assistant that helps you create the perfect desk setup. Headphone stands, monitor risers, and controller mounts are just a few examples of how you can keep your workspace clutter-free and easily accessible. These custom accessories are made just for you, based on your desk layout and personal style.

You can also create your own storage solutions for all your PC components - think bins, racks, or holders that fit perfectly with your screws, cables, and other essentials. This way, everything is organized and safe, so you never have to worry about losing something important.

You can customize everything to fit your workspace perfectly. With user-friendly modeling software, you can ensure that each design is exactly the right size and shape for your desk. Plus, you can change the color, shape, and finish to match your PC and peripherals. This way, your workspace will look like a piece of art you’ve created yourself.

The best part is that you can easily change your designs as your needs change. You'll be able to add slots for new devices or make other changes that you think would be cool.

3 Explore new personalization and esthetics

Show off distinctive themes and custom decorative elements

PC enthusiasts love to show off their unique builds. A 3D printer lets you make cool fan grills, front-panel logos, or fancy accents that match your favorite colors or lighting.

Printing new panels or cool mods can give an old PC a new look. You can make anything you want, from simple accents to complex designs. Even a tiny detail can make your PC stand out.

You can try making more complex shapes or adding layers as you get better. This makes your PC even more unique and distinct from everyone else’s. You might even decide to print and build your own ergonomic mouse!

2 Design custom parts and adapters

Improve hardware mounting, airflow control, and overall stability

With a 3D printer, you can build your own parts exactly how you want them. Imagine making fan mounts, shrouds, and cable guides that fit perfectly into your case or motherboard. This is especially useful if you have a unique setup or want to make your PC run faster.

You can also print custom adapters to guide airflow, making your PC cooler and quieter. If you are concerned about GPU sag, you can make supports that match your GPU and case, keeping it straight and stable.

By making your own parts, you can save money and make your PC look and perform better than with pre-made components.

1 Enjoy cost savings and convenience

Avoid paying premiums and waiting for specialized parts

Specialized case components and modding accessories can be pricey or hard to find, especially if they’re no longer made. However, with a 3D printer, you can avoid high prices and supply issues. Just design what you want and print it right away. No more waiting for shipping or wondering if you’ll get the needed parts.

If you find a cool mod online or make one yourself, you can print it whenever you want without worrying about running out. Sure, the printer is an initial investment, but it’ll pay for itself in the long run. Printing your own stuff over and over saves you money, and you can always change your mind and make changes to the design as you go.

Final thoughts for dedicated PC enthusiasts to consider

A 3D printer gives you a magical toolbox that lets you build anything you can imagine. It’s both creative and practical, and it can help you fix broken parts and make your own creations for your PC. Once you start printing your own PC accessories and upgrades, you’ll be amazed at how much you can improve your hardware and design skills.

Imagine having a magical tool that can turn your wildest creative ideas into physical objects in just a few hours. With a 3D printer, you can sketch your design, print it, and watch it come to life right before your eyes. This instant feedback speeds up your development process and helps you make the best decisions.

But here’s the cool part: you can keep changing and improving your design as you go. This hands-on experimentation with prototypes feels like playing with toys but can lead to some seriously innovative solutions. For example, you could try different cable routes or find a better way to mount radiators.

This practical experience also makes you a better 3D modeler. With each iteration and redesign, you learn more and more about how things work together. This practical learning experience builds confidence and helps you create better parts over time. So, if you’re up for a challenge, a 3D printer is your ticket to unlocking your creativity and making amazing things happen.

5 Replace broken parts with ease

Extend the life of components no longer in production

It can be a real pain when tiny plastic parts break on old cases or peripherals, and replacements are hard to find. However, a 3D printer can save the day. It lets you make your own stuff, like hinges, clips, or drive bay adapters that manufacturers have stopped making.

Printing your own spare parts is way faster than searching for used ones or waiting for them to ship. Once you have a good model, you can print it repeatedly, which is especially helpful if multiple parts have the same problem.

Many hobbyists keep a collection of replacement parts on hand to be ready for unexpected repairs. You can find or share these files online, which helps keep e-waste down, since there won’t be as many parts manufactured.

4 Build helpful desk and gaming accessories

Keep your workspace organized with unique printed solutions

Imagine having a 3D printer that goes beyond making simple PC upgrades. Think of your 3D printer as a personal design assistant that helps you create the perfect desk setup. Headphone stands, monitor risers, and controller mounts are just a few examples of how you can keep your workspace clutter-free and easily accessible. These custom accessories are made just for you, based on your desk layout and personal style.

You can also create your own storage solutions for all your PC components - think bins, racks, or holders that fit perfectly with your screws, cables, and other essentials. This way, everything is organized and safe, so you never have to worry about losing something important.

You can customize everything to fit your workspace perfectly. With user-friendly modeling software, you can ensure that each design is exactly the right size and shape for your desk. Plus, you can change the color, shape, and finish to match your PC and peripherals. This way, your workspace will look like a piece of art you’ve created yourself.

The best part is that you can easily change your designs as your needs change. You'll be able to add slots for new devices or make other changes that you think would be cool.

3 Explore new personalization and esthetics

Show off distinctive themes and custom decorative elements

PC enthusiasts love to show off their unique builds. A 3D printer lets you make cool fan grills, front-panel logos, or fancy accents that match your favorite colors or lighting.

Printing new panels or cool mods can give an old PC a new look. You can make anything you want, from simple accents to complex designs. Even a tiny detail can make your PC stand out.

You can try making more complex shapes or adding layers as you get better. This makes your PC even more unique and distinct from everyone else’s. You might even decide to print and build your own ergonomic mouse!

2 Design custom parts and adapters

Improve hardware mounting, airflow control, and overall stability

With a 3D printer, you can build your own parts exactly how you want them. Imagine making fan mounts, shrouds, and cable guides that fit perfectly into your case or motherboard. This is especially useful if you have a unique setup or want to make your PC run faster.

You can also print custom adapters to guide airflow, making your PC cooler and quieter. If you are concerned about GPU sag, you can make supports that match your GPU and case, keeping it straight and stable.

By making your own parts, you can save money and make your PC look and perform better than with pre-made components.

1 Enjoy cost savings and convenience

Avoid paying premiums and waiting for specialized parts

Specialized case components and modding accessories can be pricey or hard to find, especially if they’re no longer made. However, with a 3D printer, you can avoid high prices and supply issues. Just design what you want and print it right away. No more waiting for shipping or wondering if you’ll get the needed parts.

If you find a cool mod online or make one yourself, you can print it whenever you want without worrying about running out. Sure, the printer is an initial investment, but it’ll pay for itself in the long run. Printing your own stuff over and over saves you money, and you can always change your mind and make changes to the design as you go.

Final thoughts for dedicated PC enthusiasts to consider

A 3D printer gives you a magical toolbox that lets you build anything you can imagine. It’s both creative and practical, and it can help you fix broken parts and make your own creations for your PC. Once you start printing your own PC accessories and upgrades, you’ll be amazed at how much you can improve your hardware and design skills.
